source.multipart_basis: Decomposition mirrors spring-driven lancing auto-injector architecture: injection-molded polycarbonate housing (two-half shell), internal spring rail, compression spring, piston carrying lancet cartridge, forward end-stop, trigger button, safety lockout sleeve, and disposable lancet cartridge interface.

full_prompt:
Design a reusable capillary-blood lancing auto-injector body aimed at point-of-care glucose diagnostics. The device is a cylindrical handheld injector 120 mm long x 18 mm diameter, actuating a disposable 28G lancet (fingerstick) with a spring-driven linear motion. The body is injection-molded polycarbonate (yield 62 MPa, E 2.2 GPa, density 1200 kg/m^3) with a stainless steel internal spring rail and a 28G lancet holder cartridge. The injector must deliver a lancet tip stroke of 1.8 mm +/- 0.2 mm into the fingertip with a contact velocity of 2.0 m/s +/- 0.2 m/s, powered by a 14 N nominal compression spring with a free length of 20 mm. Pass/fail criteria: (R1) housing max von Mises stress during the post-fire end-stop impact of the piston at 2.0 m/s against the forward end-stop must not exceed 41 MPa (PC yield 62 / SF 1.5), (R2) actuation-button breakaway force to fire the injector must be 4-8 N per ISO 11608-1 sharps safety human-factors band, (R3) the lancet must be retracted fully inside the housing within 50 ms of firing and must physically not be re-fireable without loading a new cartridge (safety lockout), (R4) drop test on the device from 1.0 m onto a hard surface (equivalent quasi-static 200 N impulse through the housing) must not produce housing max von Mises above 41 MPa and must retain the lancet, and (R5) total device mass must not exceed 30 g per commercialization ergonomic target. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 assembly with housing, spring, piston, cartridge interface, and trigger as sub-bodies plus a verification report listing housing stress, actuation force, retraction time, drop result, and total mass.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
This design is delivered as a multi-part assembly rather than a single solid body. The decomposition comprises (A) housing_shell_top_half, (B) housing_shell_bottom_half, (C) internal_spring_rail, (D) compression_spring, (E) piston, (F) forward_end_stop, (G) trigger_button, (H) safety_lockout_sleeve, (I) lancet_cartridge_interface. The final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only — per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design a reusable capillary-blood lancing auto-injector body as a multi-part injection-molded assembly (housing top half, housing bottom half, internal stainless spring rail, compression spring, piston, forward end-stop, trigger button, safety lockout sleeve, lancet cartridge interface).

Body 120 mm long x 18 mm diameter; disposable 28G lancet; stroke 1.8 mm +/- 0.2 mm at contact velocity 2.0 m/s +/- 0.2 m/s; 14 N compression spring, 20 mm free length. Polycarbonate housing yield 62 MPa, E 2.2 GPa, density 1200 kg/m3.

Load cases: LC1 end-stop impact at 2.0 m/s; LC2 actuation breakaway 4-8 N per ISO 11608-1; LC3 retraction within defined time with safety lockout; LC4 1.0 m drop (quasi-static 200 N impulse).

Pass/fail: housing von Mises at LC1 <= 41 MPa (yield 62 / 1.5); actuation breakaway within 8 N; drop von Mises <= 41 MPa; total device mass <= 30 g; FEM at assembled level; connection DCR <= 1.0; mate-line gap <= 0.2 mm at LC1.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Spring-to-piston velocity and contact-velocity envelope are closed-form energy-conservation, not FEA.

prompt.title: Reusable capillary lancing auto-injector (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Design a 120 mm x 18 mm reusable PC body lancing auto-injector with 1.8 mm +/- 0.2 mm stroke at 2.0 m/s contact velocity, 4-8 N actuation breakaway, safety lockout, 1 m drop survival, and 30 g ergonomic cap. [Multi-part assembly] housing_shell_top_half (x1), housing_shell_bottom_half (x1), internal_spring_rail (x1), compression_spring (x1), piston (x1), forward_end_stop (x1), trigger_button (x1), safety_lockout_sleeve (x1), lancet_cartridge_interface (x1). FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under governing load case.

boundary_conditions.body: held by user at grip section

objective.primary: pass ISO 11608-1 sharps safety + stroke envelope

parts.name: housing_shell_top_half
parts.role: upper clamshell polycarbonate housing holding spring rail and trigger bezel
parts.material_hint: Polycarbonate, yield 62 MPa, E 2.2 GPa, density 1200 kg/m^3

parts.name: housing_shell_bottom_half
parts.role: lower clamshell polycarbonate housing mating to A via snap-fit + ultrasonic weld

parts.name: internal_spring_rail
parts.role: stainless steel guide rail that constrains piston linear motion and reacts spring lateral loads

parts.name: compression_spring
parts.role: 14 N nominal compression spring driving piston to 2.0 m/s contact velocity

parts.name: piston
parts.role: carriage that holds the lancet cartridge interface and translates spring energy to 2 m/s contact stroke

parts.name: forward_end_stop
parts.role: reinforced housing boss that decelerates piston at end of stroke; governs R1 impact stress
parts.material_hint: PC co-molded with housing A/B, yield 62 MPa

parts.name: trigger_button
parts.role: user actuation button delivering 4-8 N breakaway force to release piston
parts.material_hint: Polycarbonate, yield 62 MPa, with silicone overmold pad

parts.name: safety_lockout_sleeve
parts.role: sleeve preventing re-fire until a new lancet cartridge is loaded; mandated by ISO 11608-1 sharps safety
parts.material_hint: Polycarbonate, yield 62 MPa, with ratchet pawl feature

parts.name: lancet_cartridge_interface
parts.role: forward nose section accepting disposable 28G lancet cartridge with tooled retention geometry

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: PC yield 62 / 1.5 under 1 m drop quasi-static equivalent

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection DCR across snap-fit + weld + threaded interfaces <= 1.0 under LC1 end-stop impact.
Housing shell mate-line (A to B) must not separate > 0.05 mm at LC1 impact to retain lancet per R4 drop test.

interfaces.load_path: clamshell halves joined by ultrasonic weld along longitudinal mate line; transmits end-stop reaction into lower half
internal spring rail C press-fits into molded boss in housing top half A
compression spring D is captured coaxially on spring rail C; reacts axial firing force
compression spring D drives piston E via captured coil seat
piston E strikes forward end-stop F at 2.0 m/s; contact is the governing R1 impact event
trigger button G snaps into molded pocket in A; releases piston E on depression
safety lockout sleeve H rides on a pin in bottom shell B; ratchet pawl prevents re-fire
piston E couples to lancet cartridge interface I with retention tabs; transfers stroke to lancet tip

fem_check_policy.rationale: Impact stress, actuation force, retraction time, and drop integrity all depend on multi-body dynamic contact between piston, spring, end-stop, and housing — only assembled model captures them.

notes.why_multi_part: A spring-driven lancing auto-injector is inherently a multi-component mechanism (clamshell housing + spring + piston + trigger + lockout + cartridge) and cannot be modeled as a monolithic body.
notes.speculation_caveats: parts[] counts and interfaces[] reflect typical commercial lancing autoinjector architecture; not derived from a specific BMEidea rulebook.
